input.headline: How Cold War foes became close colleagues — until toilets got in the way

raw.headline: How Cold War foes became close colleagues — until toilets got in the way

winner: During the height of the Cold War, American and Soviet scientists were collaborating on a space project so top-secret that they had to share a single toilet with no partitions. The Americans were thrilled when their Soviet colleague, Ivan, finally broke the ice: "You know, comrade, I think we can end this Cold War thing if we just flush together." But then came the moment of truth—literally. The first time the American tried to use it while Ivan was in there, he froze solid with horror. Ivan smirked and said, *"Ah, the capitalism! Always afraid of a little shared resources."*

candidates.text: During the height of the Cold War, American and Soviet scientists were collaborating on a space project so top-secret that they had to share a single toilet with no partitions. The Americans were thrilled when their Soviet colleague, Ivan, finally broke the ice: "You know, comrade, I think we can end this Cold War thing if we just flush together." But then came the moment of truth—literally. The first time the American tried to use it while Ivan was in there, he froze solid with horror. Ivan smirked and said, *"Ah, the capitalism! Always afraid of a little shared resources."*
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and ties well to the headline with a surprising twist involving shared resources and Cold War tensions. The humor arises from the absurdity of the situation and the witty dialogue between characters."}

candidates.text: During a friendly Cold War debriefing, the Soviet and American delegations agreed on everything—until one American engineer asked if they could test their toilet paper holder by pulling the handle on the Soviet bidet. The Russians, who had been nodding along for hours, suddenly slammed their hands down and said, "Comrade, we do not negotiate about the flush.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "Surprising twist on Cold War diplomacy with specific historical context and toilet humor"}

candidates.text: During a thawing of Cold War tensions, American and Soviet scientists finally agreed on one thing: their toilets were terrible. "Let's team up," they said, "and flush this problem down together.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and ties the Cold War theme with a modern, relatable issue (toilets). The surprise comes from the unexpected focus on toilets as a point of collaboration, and it uses natural English effectively."}

candidates.text: The Cold War was over before the toilet negotiations started.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks specificity and surprise. It's a bit generic and doesn't fully engage with the toilet element mentioned in the headline."}

candidates.text: Why did the Soviet and American scientists stop collaborating? Because one of them kept flushing their shared bathroom into the other's half!
